lede: A look at July 28 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Malik Nabers and Zach Parise, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2022 Appalachian floods
events.desc: Catastrophic floods devastate Eastern Kentucky, resulting in 45 fatalities and causing damage to thousands of homes and businesses.

events.title: Provisional Irish Republican Army
events.desc: The Provisional Irish Republican Army calls an end to its thirty-year-long armed campaign against British rule in Northern Ireland.

events.title: 1976 Tangshan earthquake
events.desc: The Tangshan earthquake measuring between 7.8 and 8.2 moment magnitude flattens Tangshan in the People's Republic of China, killing 242,769 and injuring 164,851.

events.title: Alpha Group
events.desc: Spetsgruppa A, Russia's elite special force, was formed.

events.title: North American B-25 Mitchell
events.desc: A U.S. Army B-25 bomber crashes into the 79th floor of the Empire State Building killing 14 and injuring 26.

events.title: Boeing B-17 Flying Fortress
events.desc: First flight of the Boeing B-17 Flying Fortress.

events.title: Australasian Antarctic Expedition
events.desc: The Australasian Antarctic Expedition began as the SY Aurora departed London.

events.title: Vinnie Ream
events.desc: At the age of 18, Vinnie Ream becomes the first and youngest female artist to receive a commission from the United States government for a statue (of Abraham Lincoln).

events.title: José de San Martín
events.desc: José de San Martín declares the independence of Peru from Spain.

events.title: Henry VIII
events.desc: Henry VIII of England marries his fifth wife, Catherine Howard.

births.name: Alexis Tsipras
births.line: Greek engineer and politician, 186th Prime Minister of Greece

births.name: Albert Sarraut
births.line: French journalist and politician, 106th Prime Minister of France (died 1962)

deaths.text: William Scranton, American captain and politician, 13th United States Ambassador to the United Nations
George Law Curry, American publisher and politician

observances.text: Christian feast day: Alphonsa Muttathupadathu (Syro-Malabar Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Johann Sebastian Bach, George Frederick Handel, Henry Purcell (Episcopal Church commemoration)
Christian feast day: Johann Sebastian Bach, Heinrich Schütz, George Frederick Handel (Lutheran commemoration)
